moduleNodes.name: Pros and Cons
moduleNodes.description: A simple balance sheet to compare the advantages and disadvantages of a technology or solution.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Evaluate the solution:

[Pros] Advantages and benefits
[Cons] Disadvantages and risks

moduleNodes.name: ADR (Architecture Decision Record)
moduleNodes.description: Captures a decision, including the context and the consequences of the choice.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Record the architectural decision:

[Context] What is the problem?
[Decision] What is the change?
[Status] Proposed/Accepted/Deprecated
[Consequences] Impact after applying the decision

moduleNodes.name: RFC (Request for Comments)
moduleNodes.description: A document used to propose a new standard or feature to get team feedback.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Propose a new feature:

[Abstract] Short summary
[Motivation] Why do we need this?
[Design] Technical details
[Drawbacks] Potential downsides

moduleNodes.name: Given-When-Then
moduleNodes.description: A framework for writing test cases or behavior specifications (BDD).
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Define the behavior:

[Given] Precondition or context
[When] The action performed
[Then] The expected outcome

moduleNodes.name: API Design (Resource-Action)
moduleNodes.description: Standard framework for documenting RESTful API endpoints.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Define the endpoint:

[Endpoint] URL and Method
[Request] Parameters and Headers
[Response] Status codes and JSON Body
[Errors] Potential error messages

moduleNodes.name: Troubleshooting (Symptom-Cause-Solution)
moduleNodes.description: Framework for documenting bugs or production issues.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Document the fix:

[Symptom] What is the user seeing?
[Cause] Why is this happening?
[Solution] How to fix or work around it

moduleNodes.name: User Stories (Role-Goal-Benefit)
moduleNodes.description: A way to describe a feature from the perspective of the end user.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Write the user story:

[As] Type of user (Role)
[IWant] The feature (Goal)
[SoThat] The value (Benefit)

moduleNodes.name: Impact Mapping
moduleNodes.description: A strategic planning technique that prevents organizations from getting lost while building products.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Map the impact:

[Goal] Why are we doing this?
[Actor] Who can help or obstruct?
[Impact] How should their behavior change?
[Deliverable] What will we build?

moduleNodes.name: C4 Model (Context-Container)
moduleNodes.description: A framework for visualizing software architecture at different levels.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Describe the architecture:

[Context] System and its users
[Container] Applications and Data stores
[Component] Internal modules
[Code] Implementation details

moduleNodes.name: Post-mortem (Incident Report)
moduleNodes.description: A framework for analyzing a system failure to prevent future occurrences.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Analyze the incident:

[Summary] What happened?
[Impact] How many users were affected?
[RootCause] Why did it happen?
[ActionItems] How will we prevent this?

moduleNodes.name: Risk Matrix (Impact-Probability)
moduleNodes.description: A tool used to assess the level of risk by considering the probability of occurrence and the severity of impact.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Assess the risk:

[Prob] Probability: How likely is it to happen?
[Impact] Severity: How bad is the consequence?
[Level] Risk Score: (Prob x Impact)
[Mitigation] Planned response

moduleNodes.name: FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis)
moduleNodes.description: A step-by-step approach for identifying all possible failures in a design, manufacturing, or assembly process.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Analyze failure modes:

[Failure] What could go wrong?
[Effect] What is the consequence?
[Cause] Why would it fail?
[Detection] How can we spot it early?

moduleNodes.name: Swiss Cheese Model
moduleNodes.description: A model used in risk analysis where defenses are layers, and failures happen when 'holes' align.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Analyze system defenses:

[Layers] Existing safety barriers
[Holes] Weaknesses in each layer
[Alignment] How the failure bypassed all layers
[Fix] New barrier design

moduleNodes.name: Bowtie Method
moduleNodes.description: A diagram that visualizes the risk from causes to consequences with a focus on barriers.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Visualize the risk scenario:

[Threat] What triggers the event?
[Event] The central loss of control
[Prevent] Barriers to stop the threat
[Recover] Barriers to minimize consequence

moduleNodes.name: Risk Response Strategies
moduleNodes.description: A framework for choosing the best way to handle a specific risk.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Choose a response strategy:

[Avoid] Eliminate the threat
[Transfer] Shift impact to a third party (e.g., insurance)
[Mitigate] Reduce probability or impact
[Accept] Acknowledge and prepare for impact

moduleNodes.name: Agile (Scrum Framework)
moduleNodes.description: An iterative approach to project management and software development that helps teams deliver value to their customers faster.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Plan the sprint:

[Backlog] Product features and requirements
[Sprint] Current work cycle tasks
[Done] Definition of done/Acceptance criteria
[Velocity] Team capacity and speed

moduleNodes.name: Waterfall Model
moduleNodes.description: A linear and sequential project management approach where each phase must be completed before the next begins.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Define project phases:

[Req] Requirements documentation
[Design] System architecture
[Impl] Coding and integration
[Ver] Testing and verification

moduleNodes.name: CALMS Framework
moduleNodes.description: A conceptual framework for assessing an organization's ability to adopt DevOps processes.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Assess DevOps maturity:

[C] Culture: People and collaboration
[A] Automation: Tools and toolchains
[L] Lean: Small batches and flow
[M] Measurement: Data and monitoring
[S] Sharing: Knowledge exchange

moduleNodes.name: CI/CD Pipeline Framework
moduleNodes.description: A method to frequently deliver apps to customers by introducing automation into the stages of app development.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Design the pipeline:

[Build] Compilation and dependencies
[Test] Unit and integration tests
[Deploy] Release to environments
[Monitor] Performance and error tracking

moduleNodes.name: Kanban Method
moduleNodes.description: A visual system for managing work as it moves through a process, focusing on continuous delivery and efficiency.
moduleNodes.defaultPrompt:
Manage workflow:

[Todo] Tasks ready to start
[InProgress] Active work
[WIP] Work-in-progress limits
[LeadTime] Time from start to finish
